# checker.run_puzzle { #puzzle_widget.checker.run_puzzle }

```python
puzzle_widget.checker.run_puzzle(lines, expected)
```

Run ``lines`` (joined with newlines) in a fresh, isolated namespace and
check whether the value of the last line equals ``expected``.

Never raises: a ``SyntaxError`` from an unparseable order, or any
exception raised while running the lines, is caught and reported via
``PuzzleResult.error`` with ``success=False`` -- both are routine while a
puzzle is still scrambled, not something callers need to guard against.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**lines**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [list of str]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The current candidate order, one statement per line.

<code>[**expected**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[object](`object`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The target value (typically a literal parsed from the ``%%puzzle`` magic's argument).

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[PuzzleResult](`puzzle_widget.checker.PuzzleResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:
